name: Joe Lovano
abstract: Joseph Salvatore Lovano (born December 29, 1952) is an American jazz multi-instrumentalist. Though best known as a tenor saxophonist, Lovano has also recorded on alto clarinet, flute and drums, amongst other instruments.

about_quotes.speaker: Ralph LaLama
about_quotes.evidence: Ralph has co-led a quartet with fellow saxophonist Joe Lovano
about_quotes.doc_title: Ralph LaLama interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 23, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: it was the first album or CD was a thing called “While We’re Young” on ECM and that was recorded after we had been playing some gigs together... and that’s called “Tactics,” which was recorded live in New York... And then there was an album called “Open Land”... that had Joe Lovano and Mark Feldman
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Bley Carla
about_quotes.evidence: Sometimes Joe Lovano, tenor. Eventually Steve Slagle, alto. These are names I remember, I saw these guys hundreds of times, and wrote for them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bley Carla — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: I took the baritone out of the case and sat down next to Joe Lovano. He was in the chair next to me.

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] You recently did a recording with Joe Lovano. That was called, "I am all for you."
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Holman
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Lovano is one, in fact I’m going to be doing a project with him later this year.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Holman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999
